creator: raimeetravel
caption:
I’m finally making this happen. No excuses this time. I have the right tools and the right mindset… but I’m still scared! #AMDPartner

My @amd laptop is the best companion for this looong journey because:

- I can use the various AI capabilities to help me study, plan my schedule, and discover the best ways to learn and practice.
- I can stay connected all over the world with high-speed while I practice and do my other work too!
- it’s light and portable so I can take it anywhere and stay charged for HOURS because of the battery life!
(Insert any more things they want me to touch on

Okay this is me telling the world so that I can’t give up! Stay tuned for my progress…

transcription.text: I feel stupid and I'm calling myself out. Traveling the world is basically my personality and it's also my job, but I can only speak English. This is me holding myself accountable. I will speak conversationally fluent French in one year, maybe less. Pourquoi francais when I've already taken 10 years of Spanish and four of German? Well, I can hardly speak either of those, so I clearly need to do something differently this time. Et j'aime bien le français. So here's how I'm going to realistically make this happen with the help of my AMD AI-powered laptop. I made a plan and I'm also using some of your guys' advice. Most importantly, I'm dedicating time every day to speak and I'm doing an immersive language school plus private tutoring. I'll also be doing a whole lot of this and I came up with my schedule for making this happen in a year using my AMD laptop. It helped me map everything out and decide what to prioritize and when. I'm so done with my excuse of, well, it'll take too long, I'll do it one day because the time passes anyways and I could have been fluent by now. I think I have the right tools this time, but I could still use any advice. So if you've learned a language as an adult, what has been the most helpful thing for you?
